# test-fsemu-cases.txt -- FS-UAE test suite for nl 1.8
# Generated by test-designer agent.
# All EXPECT: values pre-computed by running native nl on macOS.
# Tabs in EXPECT: lines are literal 0x09 characters.
# The ARexx harness uses STRIP() which removes leading/trailing spaces (0x20)
# but leaves tabs intact, so EXPECT: values omit leading spaces.

# === CATEGORY 1: Functional tests -- one per documented flag ===

# Native: nl test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: Default behavior numbers non-empty body lines
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -b a test-multiline.txt (LOCAL file: 6 lines, 3 are blank)
# Line 6 is "UPPERCASE LINE" (numbered 6 with -b a)
TEST: -b a numbers all lines including blank lines
CMD: WORK:nl -b a WORK:test-multiline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_LINE: 6,6	UPPERCASE LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -b n test-oneline.txt
# OpenBSD nl unnumbered lines: 6 spaces + content (NO separator/tab)
# macOS nl unnumbered lines: 6 spaces + tab + content (always includes sep)
# STRIP("      hello world") = "hello world"
TEST: -b n suppresses all body line numbering
CMD: WORK:nl -b n WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -b t test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -b t numbers only non-empty lines (explicit default)
CMD: WORK:nl -b t WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -b p'^a' test-nl-regex.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -b p regex numbers lines matching pattern
CMD: WORK:nl -b p^a WORK:test-nl-regex.txt
EXPECT: 1	apple
EXPECT_LINE: 2,banana
EXPECT_LINE: 3,2	apricot
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -h a -f a test-nl-sections.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
# OpenBSD nl resets line counter at EVERY section transition (header, body, footer)
# unlike macOS nl which only resets at new page start (\:\:\: header)
# Port uses OpenBSD source so all section-crossing expected values use OpenBSD behavior.
TEST: -h a numbers header lines and counter resets at each section
CMD: WORK:nl -h a -f a WORK:test-nl-sections.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEADER LINE
EXPECT_LINE: 2,1	BODY LINE 1
EXPECT_LINE: 4,1	FOOTER LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -f a test-nl-sections.txt
# HEADER LINE: unnumbered section (default header=none): "      HEADER LINE"
# STRIP = "HEADER LINE" (no tab prefix -- OpenBSD never appends sep for unnumbered)
# BODY LINE 1: numbered 1 (restart at body section): "     1\tBODY LINE 1"
# FOOTER LINE: numbered 1 (restart at footer section): "     1\tFOOTER LINE"
TEST: -f a numbers footer lines with reset at footer section start
CMD: WORK:nl -f a WORK:test-nl-sections.txt
EXPECT: HEADER LINE
EXPECT_LINE: 2,1	BODY LINE 1
EXPECT_LINE: 4,1	FOOTER LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -n ln test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -n ln produces left-justified numbers
CMD: WORK:nl -n ln WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1     	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -n rn test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -n rn produces right-justified numbers without leading zeros
CMD: WORK:nl -n rn WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -n rz test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -n rz produces right-justified numbers with leading zeros
CMD: WORK:nl -n rz WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 000001	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -w 3 test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -w 3 uses 3-character-wide line number field
CMD: WORK:nl -w 3 WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -w 3 -n rz test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -w 3 -n rz produces 3-digit zero-padded numbers
CMD: WORK:nl -w 3 -n rz WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 001	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -v 10 test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -v 10 starts numbering at 10
CMD: WORK:nl -v 10 WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 10	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -v -5 test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -v negative start number works correctly
CMD: WORK:nl -v -5 WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: -5	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -i 2 test-multiline.txt (LOCAL: 6 lines, 3 are blank)
# Numbered: line1=1, line4=3, line6=5 (increment 2, blank lines not numbered)
TEST: -i 2 increments line numbers by 2
CMD: WORK:nl -i 2 WORK:test-multiline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_LINE: 4,3	foo bar baz
EXPECT_LINE: 6,5	UPPERCASE LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -s : test-oneline.txt | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -s custom separator replaces default tab
CMD: WORK:nl -s : WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT:      1:hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -l 3 -b a test-nl-consec-blanks.txt
# 3 consecutive blanks: only the 3rd blank is numbered (adjblank count reaches nblank)
# Line 1: 1\tA, Line 2-3: unnumbered blanks, Line 4: 2\t(blank), Line 5: 3\tB
# Skip asserting blank lines directly (trailing tab makes comparison tricky)
# Key assertions: first line (A=1), last line (B=3)
TEST: -l 3 counts 3 consecutive blank lines as one before numbering
CMD: WORK:nl -l 3 -b a WORK:test-nl-consec-blanks.txt
EXPECT: 1	A
EXPECT_LINE: 5,3	B
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Native: nl -p -h a -b a test-nl-twopages.txt | tail -2 | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: -p suppresses line number reset at logical page boundaries
CMD: WORK:nl -p -h a -b a WORK:test-nl-twopages.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEAD1
EXPECT_LINE: 4,4	HEAD2
EXPECT_LINE: 5,5	BODY3
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -d @@ -h a -f a test-nl-custom-delim.txt
# Custom delim @@ -> header=@@@@@@, body=@@@@, footer=@@
# OpenBSD nl resets at each section. Output: 1\tHEADER, 1\tBODY LINE, 1\tFOOTER
TEST: -d custom delimiter changes section detection character pair
CMD: WORK:nl -d @@ -h a -f a WORK:test-nl-custom-delim.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEADER
EXPECT_LINE: 2,1	BODY LINE
EXPECT_LINE: 3,1	FOOTER
EXPECT_RC: 0

# === CATEGORY 2: Logical page section tests ===

# Ported binary: nl -h a -f a test-nl-sections.txt
# OpenBSD nl resets at each section boundary. Output: 4 lines total.
# Line 1: 1\tHEADER LINE, Line 2: 1\tBODY LINE 1, Line 3: 2\tBODY LINE 2, Line 4: 1\tFOOTER LINE
TEST: Logical page header and footer sections are numbered with -h a -f a
CMD: WORK:nl -h a -f a WORK:test-nl-sections.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEADER LINE
EXPECT_LINE: 3,2	BODY LINE 2
EXPECT_LINE: 4,1	FOOTER LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -h a -b a test-nl-twopages.txt
# twopages has \:\:\: HEAD1 \:\: BODY1 BODY2 \:\:\: HEAD2 \:\: BODY3
# Output: 5 lines (all delimiter lines consumed)
# Line 1: 1\tHEAD1, Line 2: 1\tBODY1, Line 3: 2\tBODY2, Line 4: 1\tHEAD2, Line 5: 1\tBODY3
TEST: Line numbers reset at second logical page header
CMD: WORK:nl -h a -b a WORK:test-nl-twopages.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEAD1
EXPECT_LINE: 2,1	BODY1
EXPECT_LINE: 3,2	BODY2
EXPECT_LINE: 4,1	HEAD2
EXPECT_LINE: 5,1	BODY3
EXPECT_RC: 0

# === CATEGORY 3: Error path tests ===

# Error: invalid flag -- stderr only, verify RC
TEST: Invalid flag prints usage and exits with error
CMD: WORK:nl -Z WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: invalid -n format value
TEST: -n with invalid format argument exits with error
CMD: WORK:nl -n xx WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: invalid -b type
TEST: -b with invalid type argument exits with error
CMD: WORK:nl -b x WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: missing file
TEST: Non-existent file argument exits with error
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:nosuchfile-does-not-exist.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: -w 0 is below minimum
TEST: -w 0 is rejected as invalid width
CMD: WORK:nl -w 0 WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: -d with 3+ character delimiter
TEST: -d with 3-character delimiter is rejected
CMD: WORK:nl -d ABC WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: -i with non-numeric value
TEST: -i with non-numeric value is rejected
CMD: WORK:nl -i abc WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# Error: multiple file arguments (nl only accepts one)
TEST: Two file arguments triggers usage error
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-oneline.txt WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT_RC: 10

# === CATEGORY 4: Edge cases ===

# Empty file
TEST: Empty file produces no output
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-empty.txt
EXPECT_RC: 0

# All-blank lines: default -b t skips blank lines but still outputs them padded
# Each blank line outputs as 6-spaces + TAB + newline (no line number)
# Using EXPECT_RC: 0 only -- the content is non-deterministic width based on -w
TEST: All-blank file with default -b t exits successfully without numbering blanks
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-nl-allblank.txt
EXPECT_RC: 0

# All-blank with -b a: numbers all two blank lines in allblank.txt
# Output: "     1\t\n     2\t\n" -- use EXPECT_CONTAINS to check numbering occurs
TEST: All-blank file with -b a assigns numbers to blank lines
CMD: WORK:nl -b a WORK:test-nl-allblank.txt
EXPECT_CONTAINS: 2
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Long line: verify nl handles >1024 char line
# Native: nl test-longline.txt | head -c 20 -> "     1\tAAAAAAAAAAAAA"
TEST: Very long line is numbered and output correctly
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-longline.txt
EXPECT_CONTAINS: 1
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Special chars file with tabs: tabs in content are preserved
# Native: nl test-special-chars.txt | head -1 | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: File with embedded tabs in content preserves tab characters
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-special-chars.txt
EXPECT: 1	line	with	tabs
EXPECT_RC: 0

# === CATEGORY 5: Amiga-specific tests ===

# WORK: volume path handling
TEST: WORK: prefixed path resolves correctly
CMD: WORK:nl -w 2 WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 1	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# -n rz with WORK: path (verifies Amiga path + number format together)
TEST: WORK: path with -n rz format flag works correctly
CMD: WORK:nl -n rz -w 4 WORK:test-oneline.txt
EXPECT: 0001	hello world
EXPECT_RC: 0

# === CATEGORY 6: Real-world and stress tests ===

# Ported binary: nl -w 3 -n rz -s : test-multiline.txt (LOCAL: 6 lines, 3 blank)
# Numbered: line1=001, line4=002, line6=003 (blank lines not numbered)
TEST: Real-world source listing format with 3-digit zero-padded numbers and colon separator
CMD: WORK:nl -w 3 -n rz -s : WORK:test-multiline.txt
EXPECT: 001:hello world
EXPECT_LINE: 6,003:UPPERCASE LINE
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Ported binary: nl -n ln -w 4 -v 5 -i 5 -s : test-multiline.txt (LOCAL: 6 lines, 3 blank)
# Note: AmigaDOS treats | as pipe operator so we use '> ' as separator instead
# Numbered: line1=5, line4=10, line6=15 (blank lines skip, increment 5 each)
TEST: Real-world diff annotation with left-aligned custom start and increment
CMD: WORK:nl -n ln -w 4 -v 5 -i 5 -s : WORK:test-multiline.txt
EXPECT: 5   :hello world
EXPECT_LINE: 4,10  :foo bar baz
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Real-world 3: multi-page document, header+body numbered, resets at each section
# Ported binary: nl -h a -b a test-nl-twopages.txt
# Output: 5 lines (delimiter lines consumed). Reset happens at each section boundary.
# Line 1: 1\tHEAD1, Line 2: 1\tBODY1, Line 3: 2\tBODY2, Line 4: 1\tHEAD2, Line 5: 1\tBODY3
TEST: Multi-page document with header numbering resets per section boundary
CMD: WORK:nl -h a -b a WORK:test-nl-twopages.txt
EXPECT: 1	HEAD1
EXPECT_LINE: 4,1	HEAD2
EXPECT_LINE: 5,1	BODY3
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Stress 1: 500-line file -- verify correct first and last line numbers
# Native: nl test-nl-stress500.txt | tail -1 | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: 500-line file is numbered completely and correctly
CMD: WORK:nl WORK:test-nl-stress500.txt
EXPECT: 1	Line number 0001 with extra content to make realistic length
EXPECT_LINE: 500,500	Line number 0500 with extra content to make realistic length
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Stress 2: 500-line file with -b a -n rz -w 3 -- all lines, zero-padded, 3 digits
# Native: nl -b a -n rz -w 3 test-nl-stress500.txt | tail -1 | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: 500-line file with zero-padded 3-digit numbers and all-lines mode
CMD: WORK:nl -b a -n rz -w 3 WORK:test-nl-stress500.txt
EXPECT: 001	Line number 0001 with extra content to make realistic length
EXPECT_LINE: 500,500	Line number 0500 with extra content to make realistic length
EXPECT_RC: 0

# Precision 1: verify line numbering arithmetic -- -v 1 -i 1 produces correct line 250
# Native: nl -v 1 -i 1 test-nl-stress500.txt | sed -n '250p' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//'
TEST: Line count arithmetic is precise at line 250 of 500
CMD: WORK:nl -v 1 -i 1 WORK:test-nl-stress500.txt
EXPECT_LINE: 250,250	Line number 0250 with extra content to make realistic length
EXPECT_RC: 0
